title: Monsoon Juicy Canadian Beef Gravy Over Noodles
description: A hearty, resinous, and juicy dish featuring beef gravy, yuzu-cure, and satvik-friendly ingredients, perfect for a Canadian-inspired monsoon meal. This recipe serves 4-6 people and can be prepared in under 1 hour.

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: gluten-free option: replace with rice flour or cornstarch

ingredients.name: Yuzu juice
ingredients.notes: use store-bought yuzu juice or substitute with a mixture of lemon and grapefruit juices

ingredients.name: Noodles
ingredients.notes: use rice noodles or gluten-free noodles

instructions: In a large pot, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the beef broth, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the yuzu juice, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Cook the noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
In a small bowl, mix together the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter and the chopped scallions.
To serve, place the cooked noodles on a plate, then spoon the beef gravy over the top. Garnish with the scallion butter.
